name: Sacher Torte
recipeInstructions:
Preheat oven to 160C (325F). Grease a 9in spring form cake tin and line the bottom with greaseproof paper.
1. In a large bowl, cream together the butter and 1/3 cup sugar until light and fluffy.
2. Add the egg yolks one at a time, beating well after each addition.
3. Melt the chocolate and stir it into the mixture along with the almond essence and hazelnuts.
4. Mix in the cocoa powder and self-raising flour until just combined.
5. In a separate bowl, whisk the egg whites until they form stiff peaks.
6. Gradually add 1/3 cup sugar to the egg whites, about a quarter at a time, and continue whisking until all is incorporated.
7. Gently fold about one-quarter of the egg white mixture into the chocolate batter to lighten it.
8. Pour this into the prepared cake tin and smooth the top with a spatula.
9. Bake for 60-70 minutes, or until set in the center when tested with a skewer.
10. Cool completely in the tin, then turn out onto a wire rack.
11. Warm the apricot jam until it is spreadable.
12. Spread the warmed jam over the cooled cake, covering the top and sides.
13. Melt the butter and chocolate together for the icing, stirring in the brandy if using.
14. Let the icing cool slightly until it has a spreading consistency.
15. Apply the icing to the top and sides of the cake.
This recipe remains true to the original while ensuring clear instructions and consistent formatting.
